task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function is correct, concise, and handles an edge case well.
trace:
**
I solved this task by iterating through the input list and maintaining a running state of the previous number. When I encounter a number that is consecutive to the previous one, I update the previous number. When I find a non-consecutive number, I append the range or single number to the result list based on whether it's part of a larger run or not. I also handle the edge case where the input list has only one element, in which case it returns that element as is.
